channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct new members: Who made it in
item.description: It was a five-hour-plus show on Saturday that helped induct the newest members of the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, KTLA 5

item.title: Voting groups that got a boost from Harris' candidacy are still working to persuade voters of color
item.description: Left-leaning voter engagement groups saw a surge in support and energy after Kamala Harris replaced President Joe Biden as the party’s White House nominee this summer.
item.dc:creator: By Ayanna Alexander, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Learning about God from dogs, Orthodox monks breed and train canines in upstate New York monastery
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, New York (AP) — After he and his fellow monks sang morning prayers in their church nestled in a forest, Brother Luke walked back to his residence to be greeted by a different kind of choir.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct Cher, Foreigner, Mary J. Blige, Ozzy and more
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Hollywood stars Julia Roberts and Zendaya bookended Saturday’s inductions into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, giving a little stardust to an eclectic lineup that included pop icon Cher, heavy metal’s Ozzy Osbourne, hip-hop soul queen Mary J.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas' junk science law is getting another look over Robert Roberson's case
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — When Robert Roberson's execution was abruptly halted in Texas, it was due to a subpoena ordering him to testify over a legal backstop that both Republicans and Democrats say should had saved him long ago: Texas’ junk science law.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man charged in NYC subway chokehold death set to stand trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — To some New Yorkers, he’s the white vigilante who choked an innocent Black man to death on the subway. To others, he’s the U.S. Marine Corps veteran whose attempt to subdue a mentally ill man ended in tragedy.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 7 dead after ferry dock gangway collapses on Georgia's Sapelo Island
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Authorities said at least seven people were killed Saturday when part of a ferry dock collapsed on Georgia's Sapelo Island, where crowds had gathered for a fall celebration by the island's tiny Gullah-Geechee community of Black slave descendants.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some spots on Mars could have the potential to support life: Study
item.description: Dust-infused ice could create enough warmth below the surface to melt, giving life a chance, say researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Kyle Larson opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs as the title favorite
item.description: Kyle Larson has been lucky of late in Las Vegas, where he opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs on top of the Cup Series standings and the favorite to win Sunday's race.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC officials envision turning Fifth Avenue into a grand boulevard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s famed luxury store row Fifth Avenue is in line for a major makeover. New York City officials unveiled a plan this week to transform a central portion of the thoroughfare between Bryant Park and Central Park into a more pedestrian-centered boulevard.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched this week by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, Fox 5

item.title: Mississippi couple celebrates 60 years of marriage at Chick-Fil-A
item.description: A Mississippi couple celebrated their 60th marriage anniversary at Chick-fil-A after eating lunch at the restaurant every day for the past two years.
item.dc:creator: Shay Simon, NewsNation

item.title: Phone bans in class wanted, but not all-day restrictions: Poll
item.description: 68% of U.S. adults said in a Pew Research Center survey conducted Sept. 30-Oct. 6, 2024 say they approve of such rules.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chumash people in California to co-steward marine sanctuary in historic partnership
item.description: For more than 10,000 years, Native Americans have been living along California’s central coast, an area of breathtaking beauty with stunning turquoise waters rich in biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The last in-person vote in the US will be cast on the desolate tundra of Alaska's Aleutian Islands
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — On a desolate slab of island tundra in western Alaska, a resident of Adak will again become the last American to cast an in-person ballot for president, continuing a 12-year tradition for the nation’s westernmost community.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, Fox 5

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Video and an unused bullet prove man's guilt in Indiana girls' killings, prosecutor says
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. (AP) — A man charged with killing two teenage girls in a small Indiana community forced them off a hiking trail before cutting their throats, a prosecutor said Friday, telling jurors that the evidence includes an unused bullet and video recorded on the eldest girl’s phone.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas was about to execute Robert Roberson. Then a last-ditch tactic bought him more time
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Robert Roberson, set to be the first person in the U.S. to be put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome, was nearly out of options to stop his execution in Texas. A state parole board, multiple lower courts and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: It's more dangerous to drive at night in these states than anywhere else in the US
item.description: Driving at night is dangerous no matter where you are in the U.S. It's harder to see and you're more likely to be sharing the road with fellow drivers who are either tired and could potentially doze off or have had one too many drinks and are driving under the influence.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Menendez brothers inch closer to freedom, Bryan Kohberger hits harsh reality
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Oklahoma parents and teachers sue to stop top education official's classroom Bible mandate
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A group of Oklahoma parents of public school students, teachers and ministers filed a lawsuit Thursday seeking to stop the state's top education official from forcing schools to incorporate the Bible into lesson plans for students in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4
